description_long: This is a classic pipe wrench featuring a robust, dark metallic head with an adjustable jaw mechanism. The wrench's handle is made of wood, showing a natural brown color with a slightly worn appearance and a metallic collar near the head. The adjustment mechanism is visible as a knurled wheel, allowing the jaws to open and close to grip pipes or fittings of various sizes. The head of the wrench is heavy and solid, with serrated jaws for a secure grip on round surfaces. There are visible signs of use, including some discoloration and minor wear on the handle and metal parts. The overall design is utilitarian, built for durability and effective manual operation in urban maintenance or repair settings.
description: A sturdy pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable head and a worn wooden handle, featuring a knurled adjustment wheel and serrated jaws. The tool shows signs of use and is designed for gripping and turning pipes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the jaws of the wrench and the adjustment wheel, with the handle receding backward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the wrench, the wooden handle, and the profile of the adjustable jaw.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden handle and the back of the wrench head.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the handle and the other profile of the wrench head and jaw.
attribute_handle_grip_pattern: The handle grip is smooth wood with visible grain and some discoloration, but no distinct pattern or knurling.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Minor wear marks and discoloration are visible on the handle and metal head, especially near the adjustment area.
